sections.paragraph:
& חֶלֶב ( Isa 34:6) with suff. חֶלְבֹּו pl. חֲלָבִים const. חֶלְבֵי Gen 4:4, m.

(1) fat, fatness, Levit. 3:3 , seq.; 4:8 , 31 , 35 metaph.-(a) the best or most excellent of any kind. חֵלֶב הָאָרֶץ the fat of the land, i.e. the best of its fruits, Gen 45:18 חֵלֶב חִטָּה Ps. 81:17 חֵלֶב חִטִּים Psa 147:14, fat of wheat, and חֵלֶב כִּלְיֹות חִטָּה Deu 32:14 (comp. Isa 34:6), fat of the kidneys of wheat, i.e. the best wheat.-(b) a fat heart, i.e. torpid, unfeeling, Psa 17:10 compare 73:7 , and Gr. παχὺς, Lat. pinguis, for foolish, stupid. Some have compared خِلْبُ pericardium, but that also seems to be so called from fatness, although under the root خلب there are in Arabic all kinds of other things.

(2) [Heleb], pr.n. of one of David’s captains, 2Sa 23:29 for which 1Ch 11:30 is חֵלֶד, and 27:19 חֶלְדַּי.
